_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in writing code in a currently supported programming language (e.g., Java, C++).
reference_document_element_description: Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature engineering, model training, and evaluation, including documentation and unit testing.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI AI domain. AI-S-003 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-001 (TF, cosine: 0.542), TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.540).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 3.7/10 (raw 0.374). Shared vocabulary: [code, skill, writing]. DCWF-only terms [c, currently, java, language, programming, supported] vs WIDAI-only terms [data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature, model] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.862 (P=0.864, R=0.861). Balanced P/R — the 13-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: mixed signal (E:0.0%, N:75.6%, C:24.4%). No dominant classification for "Skill in writing code in a currently supported programming language (e.g., Java," vs "Skill in writing production-quality Python code for data processing, feature eng" — the relationship is genuinely ambiguous from a logical inference perspective.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.610 > STS 0.374 (gap +0.236). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [code, skill, writing] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.8%. Shared tokens: [code, skill, writing]. DCWF-only: [c, currently, java, language, programming, supported]. WIDAI-only: [data, documentation, engineering, evaluation, feature, model].
